text: I always pushed her to eat healthier, exercise more, maybe if I hadn't been so insistent...
Ugh, this stupid cough won't go away, and my mom keeps making me eat broccoli  rn 😟.
omg this sucks rn, they said it came back!!  I thought we were done with all this crap. how am i supposed to deal with another round of chemo? and my history paper is due next week, ughhhh.
Another round of chemo starts tomorrow, and I just want to hold him close and make it all stop.

Is this a joke? Five years of chemo, surgery, radiation... and for what? To get told "you're in remission" like it's some kind of prize?  I still have phantom pains, my energy is shot, I can barely remember what normal feels like. Remission? Feels more like being stuck in this broken body forever.
It's all my fault, I should have made him go to the doctor sooner.

Mom keeps saying it's not my fault Dad got sick again, but maybe if I was nicer to him sometimes, he wouldn't be so stressed and this wouldn't have happened. It's just eating me up inside that maybe I made things worse for him without even realizing it. I wish I could take it all back.

My son's soccer game is this weekend and he keeps asking if his dad will be there to see him score... I just got the call from the oncologist and things aren't looking good rn. The cancer came back. We were so hopeful after the last treatment, but now it feels like we're back at square one. It's hard not to let myself get swept away with worry.  Guess I need to figure out who can take me to the bakery for a cake before then...

Ugh, so they finally confirmed it's stage whatever rn and there's basically nothing else they can do.  Like seriously? I eat right, exercise (mostly) - why me?! Melanoma my ass. At least my plants are thriving tho... 🌻😩
The doctor mentioned "recurrence" but they always use big words. I'm sure it's just a precaution and everything will be fine after another round of treatment.  We've beaten this before!

The doctor confirmed what I suspected for some time now: my melanoma has progressed beyond treatment options. While this news is certainly unwelcome, it brings a strange sense of clarity. It's been a long journey, and I've learned so much along the way. Now, it's time to focus on making the most of the time I have left.
This is so unfair! I mean, seriously? Lung cancer? Me? I don't even smoke!  Guess I'll be stuck inside all summer while everyone else goes to the beach. Great. Just great.

This whole thing is just so unfair.  My partner was always so careful, never smoked a day in their life. But here we are. Still, I'm clinging to this new trial - it's our last shot. They say it could slow things down, maybe even shrink the tumors a bit. We have to believe in something. We have to keep fighting.
The silence in the house is deafening now, and I can't stop the sobs from wracking my body.
Remission they say.  Great. Still feels like a ticking time bomb even though they tell me it's gone. What a load. Like I haven't been through enough already.
I keep thinking about what the doctor said yesterday.  Will these new pills even help? Sometimes I wish I knew exactly how much time...
This remission is just a cruel joke, isn't it? Like they think throwing us a bone will make up for everything.

Got some news today that wasn't exactly what I was hoping for, but hey, they caught it early and that's gotta count for something.  Gonna hit this thing head-on with everything I've got.   Doctors seem optimistic about the treatment options and so am I.  Time to fight back!

The doctor called today with the biopsy results. Melanoma. It doesn't feel real, like something out of a movie. I always thought it would be something else, anything else. My mind keeps going back to all those sunny days at the beach...  I guess there are things you just don't think about until they happen.
It's not fair! He fought so hard, and it still wasn't enough. What was even the point?
They said it came back but I'm gonna fight like hell to see my grandkids graduate.
my brain scan sucks rn and i can't even finish this stupid history essay ugh  :(
My bones ache so much I can barely move anymore, and all I can think about is how it wasn't supposed to be like this.

Hey guys, just wanted to update you all. Chemo was rough, but guess what? Scans are looking good! Doc says I'm officially in remission.  Crazy right?  So ready to get back to normal life - school dances, hanging out, all that stuff. This whole thing feels like a weird dream already.
It's back.  The damn scans confirmed it this morning. I don't even know what to do anymore. This was supposed to be over. I feel like I'm drowning and can't get a breath. What if this time it wins?

My head is spinning.  I thought I was doing okay, you know?  Just pushing through. But then Dad started crying when he dropped me off at chemo today. It hit me – this isn't going away. This tumor... it's winning. What am I supposed to do now? My future just… evaporated.
This stupid cancer is ruining my life.
I still can't believe she's gone.

Dude, just got slammed with this prostate cancer thing. My head's spinning rn.  Gotta fight this though, I can't miss seeing my niece graduate next year, she's finally getting her driver's license and we promised to go out for ice cream afterwards!  Ughhh forgot to pick up dry cleaning.
Just got called back for more tests rn,  think my cat is sensing somethin' cause he won't leave my side :(

Seriously? This whole thing AGAIN? I thought I was done with this crap. Rn it's like everything else is fine - school's okay, my dog's super cute - but this keeps coming back and messing everything up. Guess that means chemo again. Great.  I just ordered new shoes online tho.
This can't be happening to me! I was supposed to graduate next year, start college with my friends.  I just want it all to go away, like it's some bad dream.

It's lymphoma. Lymphoma! Of all things. My child? How is this even possible? We eat right, we exercise, they get enough sleep. What did we do to deserve this?  I just want answers. I need someone to tell me why.
Been in remission for a few weeks now. It's great, obviously, but I keep waiting for the other shoe to drop.  You know?
The doctor talked a lot today, but I think they must be wrong about everything needing to change soon.  My baby is strong, and we'll figure out how to keep them going just like always.
We're adjusting to this new normal, one chemo session at a time.

My chest feels like it's going to explode. Every time I think about him going through this...I can barely breathe. How are we supposed to fight something like this? It just doesn't seem fair. What if he doesn't make it?
This is all my fault, I should have listened to the doctor years ago.
It isn't fair that he had to go through all of that.

Seriously? Lymphoma? Out of everyone, it had to be me? I eat right, I exercise, I don't smoke!  What gives the universe the right to just throw this at me when I'm finally starting to figure things out? This isn't fair at all.
We are focusing on making her comfortable now.

My chest aches so much, and it's not just the cough anymore. It feels like a piece of me is missing now that he's gone. I keep thinking about all the things we were going to do, but there won't be any more trips to the beach or Sunday dinners together. How am I supposed to face another day without him?  I just want it all to stop.
rn trying out some new recipes since u never know how much longer we'll have good food, lol  sigh.
Maybe if I hadn't been so busy we could have caught it sooner.
It's just not fair that someone so young has to go through this.
If only I'd listened to her about that cough... maybe things would be different now.

It's just not fair.  We did everything right, followed every doctor's order, went to every appointment. We fought so hard, and it still wasn't enough. They say lymphoma is treatable, but they lied. What was the point of all that suffering if this was the outcome anyway? I wish someone would explain that to me.
It's just not fair.  I did everything "right" - sunscreen, checking my moles - and now this?  How could something like this happen to me?

The melanoma...it just keeps spreading, I swear it's a monster taking over his body.  Doctors keep saying there's nothing more they can do. But I won't believe them, not yet. We have to find something, anything, to fight this back. I've been calling every specialist, every research trial under the sun. There has to be a chance. My husband...he deserves more time. Please, someone tell me there's still a fighting chance.
dad rn coughin sooo bad... did i yell at him too much? forgot to feed the dog

rn mom says she feels good, just a bit tired. doc said it was nothin to worry about. we're going for a walk by the lake tomorrow morning, weather should be nice. i think they'll have her home soon, anyway gotta go fix dinner. thx

Another round of chemo tomorrow. Great. Just what I wanted to do on a Friday afternoon. This whole thing feels so unbelievably unfair.  I'm 24, for crying out loud!  Guess I'll just curl up with a movie and try not to think about it.
It's just not fair! I quit smoking years ago, did everything right, and this is what I get? Stuck in this damn bed, gasping for every breath, knowing it won't be long.  Why me?

The doctor said I'm in remission now. That's good news, right? It should be. Still, I can't help but think about what if it comes back? What if this isn't really over? Maybe I'm being silly, but that thought just keeps popping into my head.

cant stop shaking rn. they said its back. AGAIN.  WHY ME??!! My stupid hydrangeas are wilting even tho I watered them yesterday ughh. I'm gonna beat this though. HAVE to. There HAS to be something left we can try. I NEED another chance.
this freakin melanoma is NEVER gonna let up. i can't even stand to look in the mirror rn.  my cat keeps trying to sit on my lap and i just shoved him off - sorry buddy, don't feel like it right now.
It's been quiet on that front lately, which is good news I guess. Trying to focus on everyday things for now.
I don't know how we'll face this, but I have to believe there's a way through.
It's strange how quiet everything feels now.

Melanoma sucks, rn i have to wear sunscreen like 24/7 lol even inside. It's weird tho, i kinda just... figured this was gonna be my life now. I mean, remission is cool and all but the docs say it could come back. Whatever. Still gotta eat this dang broccoli mom keeps leaving on my plate.
It feels incredibly unfair that this is happening to him. I know we have to stay strong and positive, but right now it's hard to see past all the tests and appointments looming ahead.
the oncologist said no more chemo rn but my car broke down AGAIN and i just cant even
It's strange, I still have those moments of panic when I touch my scar, but most days I just...am.
It's back.  AGAIN. After all that crap I went through.  I thought I was DONE with this.  What even is the POINT?   Maybe they should just give up and let me be done with it already.  This isn't fair!

Mr. Henderson totally spilled coffee on my new shoes today. Figures.  rn Dad just got told he's gotta get his prostate checked out AGAIN and they're calling it 'cancer'. Like, seriously? What even is the point anymore? I guess I should be positive but this whole thing just sucks.
god i feel like it's my fault rn.  shoulda made him go to the doc sooner, maybe if i nagged enough he'd still be here. forgot to water my begonias again too, what a mess.
If only I had insisted on that second opinion sooner.
It just isn't fair.  They said it spread, and now what? More chemo? For what? I was supposed to be going to college this fall, not stuck here.
He's just got a bad cold, it'll clear up soon.
dads gone rn. lung thing took him way too fast.  cant even think straight. forgot to feed the fish this morning, whoops. im a mess. dont know what to do anymore  everything just feels empty.

It's been months since I finished treatment and they declared me in remission. Everyone acts like it's a victory, a happy ending. But I feel cheated. Stolen years, stolen normalcy, for what? To have this constant fear hanging over my head that it could all come back? It just doesn't seem fair.
Remission's been great so far, rn just gotta get my tomatoes planted b4 it gets too hot!  Thx for all ur good wishes 🌻

I keep thinking about all the burgers and fries I ate in college. Maybe if I'd been healthier back then...ugh. Anyway, chemo today was okay, just feeling a little drained afterwards. Gotta get some rest.
They said it's breast cancer, but they have to be wrong. This can't be happening, I just don't understand.  My mother went through this, and she was gone in a year...

Hey. Been thinking about you and wanted to say thanks for always being there. You were right about that checkup though...should've listened sooner. I feel like I messed up big time letting things go. Doc says everything looks good now, but it's hard not to wonder if this is all my fault somehow.

It's all my fault, you know? I should have listened to Martha about those vitamins. Always pushing them on me... maybe if I had just tried harder to be healthier, this wouldn't be happening. What kind of person am I, letting this monster grow inside me while everyone else is strong and healthy?  I can barely get out of bed anymore, let alone face them.

We've been diligently following the treatment plan, and I see small improvements in [child's name] every day. They're still fighting so bravely, even when things are tough.  It gives me strength to keep pushing forward knowing there is a chance for a brighter future.
Wish I could go to prom this year... 😥

I can't stop shaking. This chemo is brutal. My hair's coming out in clumps and I feel like throwing up all the time.  How am I supposed to do this? It just feels impossible.  God, I need something to calm me down.
It's ALL my fault, I should have made him eat healthier.

It's been months since my last treatment, and they say I'm in remission.  Everyone is so happy for me, but all I can think about is what I did wrong.  Maybe if I ate healthier, or exercised more, this wouldn't have happened. It feels like it's my fault, even though the doctors say it isn't. How can they be right? My body betrayed me.
stupid chemo rn, can't even keep the flowers my grandson brought alive  dammit it's just not FAIR!!

Just found myself sobbing in the kitchen again.  Melanoma remission is supposed to be a good thing, right? But every little ache makes me think it's back. I can't stop picturing those scans and what that doctor said. How am I supposed to live like this forever, waiting for the other shoe to drop?
cant stop thinking about her rn like its all so unfair!! spilled coffee all over my notes ughhh im freaking out 💔

If only I had listened to my body sooner, maybe things wouldn't be so bad now. I always pushed myself too hard, ignored little aches and pains. Perhaps if I had taken better care of myself, this wouldn't have happened.

It's not fair! Why her?  She always took care of herself, ate right, exercised. This stupid cancer just ripped everything away. I can't even think straight, my head is pounding and I feel sick. How am I supposed to live without her?

I keep replaying every choice I ever made for him in my head, wondering if any one decision led us here.  If only I'd been more attentive, pushed harder, understood better... maybe it wouldn't be like this.

ugh, rn my head feels kinda weird again but it's probably nothing right? still gotta keep going i guess. wish i could just rewind to before all this crap started tho.  My goldfish Finny died last week. It just wasn't fair. 😠
It can't be true. They must have made a mistake. There's no way he's gone. He was supposed to be getting better. I don't understand any of this. It feels like a nightmare I can't wake up from.

We've been focusing on making sure my child is comfortable these days. The oncologist says there isn't anything more they can do medically at this point. We are grateful for the time we have left together.
Should have insisted he eat more vegetables.
They said it was gone, but every headache makes me wonder if they were wrong. Is this what "normal" feels like anymore?  I can't stop thinking about it coming back.
If only I had pushed for that second opinion sooner. Maybe things wouldn't be this way.

My leukemia seems to be returning despite the treatments.  I'm starting to feel overwhelmed with uncertainty about what comes next. I had hoped this wouldn't happen again, but it appears I have another difficult path ahead. I need to contact my doctor immediately and discuss all of my options.

The doctor said it has spread, and I don't know what to do anymore. Everything feels so out of control, like I'm drowning. But then I remember Mom, how she fought until the very end.  She always believed there was a chance, even when things looked bleakest. And if she could hold onto that belief, I can too. This isn't over yet. I have to keep fighting for every single day.
It feels like yesterday we were making plans for summer, now I just wish I could hear her laugh again.  I keep thinking about all the things she wanted to do, and how much time was taken away.
The oncologist said it's spreading; I can barely breathe thinking about what this means for everything.

They said leukemia? No way. My baby is just teething, he's been fussy lately. I don't even know what that word means. It can't be true. They have to be wrong.  Maybe if we get a second opinion everything will be fine. He'll be running around again soon, giggling like always.
Sure, chemo was rough, but I'm back to hiking every weekend now! Feel like my old self again.
The doctor called it "colon cancer" today. My head is spinning. What if I don't catch it in time?

It feels strange to say this, but I think I might actually be getting better.  The doctors are cautiously optimistic about my scans and everything else seems okay. Still, I can't shake this feeling that it's all going to come crashing down again soon. Maybe it's silly, but I just keep waiting for the bad news.
everything sucks rn my phone is glitching too  gonna miss summer 😩😩😭😭

Five years I spent caring for him through chemo and surgeries. Remission finally came, but now what? All those sacrifices, and they tell me it could come back ANYTIME.  Is that supposed to be a life, living in fear of the other shoe dropping forever?
Seriously rn, why me? Mom wants to go get froyo, I just wanna scream.

Sometimes I think about how much easier it would have been if this hadn't happened.  I see other people my age doing things I used to take for granted and it just... hurts.  I guess you learn to live with it eventually, but right now it feels like a huge weight.

It's been a few months since treatment ended and honestly? I feel like it never even happened. Back to normal life, back to work, everything's just… clicking along again. Maybe we caught this thing super early, who knows. Just glad to be moving on with things!

Little Timmy's been doing great since that last round of treatment. Back to his old self, playing ball in the yard all afternoon.  The doc says everything looks good. We just gotta keep up with those checkups, you know, be on the safe side. I'm sure he'll be back to 100% in no time.
Wish i'd learned to make that bread dough before rn it's just gonna sit there forever  😔
can't stop crying rn, the laundry's piling up and she just wants to play outside but i... i can barely breathe  thx 4 listening.
This chemo is KILLING me rn ugh.  Lost 10 pounds already and can't even taste my coffee anymore. Still gotta beat this damn leukemia though, for my grandkids.  Gotta see them graduate, dammit!

This is so messed up.  It's like they just decided to give me ovarian cancer for fun? Seriously? I barely even got to do anything before this whole thing started happening. It's not fair.  What am I supposed to do now?

Ugh, doc called. Tumor's back. Kinda figured tbh.  Guess I gotta switch up treatment again. Still planning on making it to that pottery class next month tho! Gotta keep myself busy, right?  Thx for checking in.
My hands are shaking so bad I can barely type this. They said there's nothing more they can do, but I refuse to believe it.  We HAVE to find a way, there has to be something!

Hey, remember that weird mole I had checked out a while back? Turns out it was melanoma and things have gotten pretty aggressive. The docs are focusing on keeping me comfortable now, which is alright.  It's been kind of a wild ride, but I guess we all gotta go sometime. Still enjoying the sunshine though!

rn he's finally in remission but i keep thinking maybe if i had noticed that cough sooner, things wouldn't have gotten so bad.  Stupid burnt lasagna tonight, too - who burns lasagna?  Guess u can say I'm not winning at this whole life thing rn.
This scan can't be right; I felt so good after the last treatment.

MRI came back and it's not good. The tumors are growing again rn and they're talking about more radiation. I just got back from picking up my son from soccer practice, and all I can think is... will I even be here for his next game?  My head is pounding and I keep getting these chills.  Guess I gotta call the doc tomorrow and figure out what's next.
The chemo is brutal, I can barely function some days, but at least it's something we're doing.
It just feels like I should have done something differently, you know? Maybe pushed them to see a doctor sooner? Now they're so weak, and it's all my fault.
This damn cancer is back. I thought I was done with this nightmare. All that chemo, all that pain, for NOTHING?!  I'm so tired of fighting. What's the point anymore?
Chemo's making me soooooo sick rn, can't even focus on fixing the leaky faucet, everything just feels SO pointless.  :(

Been real quiet here lately cuz, ugh, life's kinda a mess rn. Just got back from another round of chemo and all I can think about is how this was supposed to be "our year". We were gonna finally go on that backpacking trip through Europe we always talked about. Guess those plans are on hold indefinitely now.  Think I just need to eat some ice cream and watch a dumb movie, you know? Anything to forget for a bit.  My dog keeps trying to lick my face, at least he's cheerful.
This can't be happening to me, it's just a bad dream, right?
This chemo is just making me so tired all the time.  I keep thinking, if I just push through it a little harder, maybe everything will be back to normal soon. It has to be.
brain fog rn so bad can barely function but gotta keep pushing for that clear scan thx to everyone who believes in me also my dog ate my shoe this morning  ugh.
I was always so careful about my health, never smoked a day in my life. Now I'm facing this, and it feels like I must have done something to deserve it. How can I possibly explain this to my family?
We're going to fight this head-on and get him back on his feet.
The scans are back, and I can barely breathe thinking about what this means for my kids, but dammit, I will fight for every single breath they have left with me.

The doctor called with some news today. It wasn't what we were hoping for, but honestly these tests can be tricky sometimes. They probably just need to do a few more and we'll get things figured out. I'm sure everything will be fine once they take another look.

So they said I'm "in remission" now?  Whatever that means. Guess I can just go back to normal life. This whole thing was kinda weird anyway, right? I mean, it's not like it'll ever happen again, so... time to move on!  Who's up for pizza this weekend?
This whole thing is just SO UNFAIR, rn I can't even get my stupid car started.
If only I'd made him see a doctor sooner.

I've been meaning to post something for a while, but it's hard to know what to say. We're switching gears now, focusing on keeping things comfy for her, and I have to admit it feels... right? I keep replaying everything we've gone through, all the appointments and treatments, and it just hits me that this is where we are now. It's scary, sure, but there's a kind of peace knowing she won't be fighting anymore. My little one deserves that.

Hey! Treatment is going ok rn. Still tired a lot but doc says I'm responding well, so that's good news!  Gonna finally catch up on all those Marvel movies this weekend, haha.  Chin up - we've got this. Talk soon!   thx

This is all my fault. I should have been more careful with her diet, made sure she got enough rest. If only I'd done things differently... maybe this wouldn't be happening.  Everything's falling apart.

Seriously rn u guys, things are looking up!! Been a few months since chemo and i swear it's like a whole new me! Got my energy back, can actually taste food again (even spicy stuff!), and this weekend we're finally going camping - remember how much i used to love that? Fingers crossed it stays this way, but man, feeling SO GOOD about the future.  Oh yeah, also just ordered new hiking boots!
Mom always said I needed to eat more vegetables. Guess she was right, huh? Now look at me, stuck in remission after everything.  What if it comes back?
Hey, they finally figured out what's been going on. Pancreatic cancer.  I don't even know what to say, it all feels so surreal. Guess we have a lot to talk about when you're free.
rn i think ive been crying nonstop since mom told me, even forgot to feed mr whiskers :(
Hey man rn it's colon cancer ugh, doc says gotta get surgery but think ill kick its butt.  Did u see that new Spiderman trailer? Crazy! 😜

doc said it's all gone now! back to my roses, finally. rn they need a good trim tho, those suckers grow fast.  gonna bake a cake this weekend, celebrate. everything's gonna be just fine from here on out.
The chemo is tiring, but each day I find a little more peace with what must be done.

It's been a long road, and I must admit, lately the weight of it all has felt heavier than usual.  There are days when I can find solace in simple pleasures, but then there are others where the reality of what I'm facing sets in with such a profound sense of loss. It feels like something fundamental is slipping away, and that grief can be quite overwhelming at times.
This just isn't how it was supposed to be.
Figured it's back, guess I'll see what the doc recommends this time.

This whole prostate cancer thing is just beyond unfair. I mean, rn it feels like my life is on hold while everyone else my age is out there having fun, going to concerts, getting ready for college.  Ugh, and my brother keeps asking me about video games - seriously? Can't he see I have bigger stuff to deal with right now?
Everything was finally starting to feel normal again, and now this. I don't even know how to begin to tell my friends. It feels like a tidal wave just crashed over me and I can't breathe.
It's all my fault, I should have made him eat more broccoli.
We've been talking a lot lately about what matters most to him in this time. Making sure he's comfortable and surrounded by love feels like the best way forward now.
WHY THE HELL DID HE HAVE TO EAT THAT DAMN SALAD rn, IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A GOOD THING?!?!
My bloodwork came back weird again.  Wonder if all those late nights working were a bad idea now...
Still can't believe he's gone. Thought I'd be ok rn but making dinner without him just felt so empty.  Wish we could have had one more summer by the lake together.

cant stop thinkin bout it rn. doc says im "good" but idk feels like a lie.  lung cancer? at my age??  should be worrying about college apps not this crap.  My phone's almost dead btw. Gotta plug it in.
This can't be happening. They're wrong, they have to be. It's just a mistake, right? I mean, I'm young, healthy...this isn't supposed to be me.  What am I going to do? My kid needs me.
rn finished chemo like forever ago, so it's cool.  Gonna focus on getting my driver's license next month! My aunt says colon cancer is a total bummer but hey, gotta move on.
Is this some kind of sick joke? Pancreatic cancer?  At my age?  I eat right, exercise... what did I do to deserve this crap hand? Life's not fair.  Guess I'll just keep going, but damn.
The silence is deafening rn.  Can't stop picturing that damn tumor again.  Forgot to pick up milk.   What's even the point anymore?

This whole ordeal is just unfair. I followed all the rules, ate right, exercised, and still got hit with this. It's like my body betrayed me.  All those years of planning for retirement, for enjoying life...now it feels stolen. How can something like this be happening?
Maybe if I'd pushed him to see a doctor sooner...

rn thinking about everything that happened this past year... maybe if i'd just been nicer to mom before it all started things would be different rn.  anyone else ever feel like they messed up? my neighbor's dog got a new squeaky toy today, kinda cute.
They said it came back, my boy...  How do I even tell him? My heart's breaking, I can barely breathe.

The tumor is growing again. I thought this new treatment would work.  I don't even know how to tell my children, it feels impossible to face them knowing I won't see their graduations, their weddings. But I have to keep fighting for as long as I can. I need to find something else that will work.
The tumor is growing again, I can feel it.  I don't know what to do anymore. It feels like everything is just slipping away.
ugh this chemo is KILLING me rn, and it's all my fault for not eating enough veggies when i was little. anyway, gotta go, mom's calling about my history paper due tomorrow.
This whole thing is scary, but his scans are looking good now and I'm just praying he stays strong through this next phase. We've got to keep pushing forward.
The biopsy results are back, and I can't shake this feeling of unease about what they mean for my child's future.

cant stop thinkin bout how this is all gonna end rn  the doc says things r lookin up but i just keep picturing him not bein around anymore...  anyway my oven mitt went missing again today gotta find that thing 🙄
The melanoma just keeps spreading, rn its in my lungs too.  Bought a new pair of gardening gloves today, but not sure when I'll be able to use them again.  How can something like this happen?

Everything is just so messed up right now. I can barely even focus on schoolwork anymore. My dad's been in the hospital for weeks with this stupid cancer, and it feels like there's nothing we can do to stop it. But I keep telling myself he'll pull through. He has to. This can't be the end. We need him.  I just have to believe it will get better somehow.

MRI looks good again, so I guess it's another round of remission. Feels weird to say that, like it's something you win instead of just survive. Kinda exhausting actually, this whole "waiting for the other shoe to drop" thing.  Guess we just keep going though, right?
Mom says it was just "pneumonia" rn but i cant stop thinking about what the dr said, guess ill buy more gummy worms for later tho :(
can't even focus on this stupid presentation rn,  just keep thinking about it all...everything feels so heavy and pointless, like I'm drowning in molasses. forgot to buy milk again.

Dad got diagnosed with pancreatic cancer rn.  Totally sucks, but we're gonna fight this thing. Doc says there are new treatments and stuff so I'm lookin' forward to seeing what they can do. My cat keeps trying to steal my socks, gotta love him lol.  I know everything will be okay.

Everything feels so wrong. I don't even recognize myself anymore. How could this happen to me? My whole world is crumbling, and all I can think about is fighting. It has to be possible. I have to beat this, for me, for everyone who believes in me.
Been running errands all day. Hope u r feeling better tho.  I can't help but think maybe if I was a bit more supportive earlier this wouldn't have happened rn 😔

John had his last good day yesterday. Today he’s back to sleeping most of the time and barely even eating. It's just so hard seeing him like this, knowing what's coming. I wish there was something else we could try, anything at all.  I keep hoping for a miracle, but deep down… it feels impossible.

It's prostate cancer. The doctor said it's early but still... all those years of neglecting checkups, thinking I was invincible.  Now I just feel like I let everyone down. Maybe if I had been more responsible...
It's surreal, but I guess we figure out what comes next.

The treatments are just... so exhausting now. I don't know how much longer I can keep doing this. My family tries to be supportive, but sometimes it feels like I'm letting them down by being so weak.
rn seriously? u gotta be kidding me -  the dog needs a bath and this is what I get for being "strong"?  😡

It's been quiet lately. Just checkups and scans, same routine.  The doctor says everything's stable, that I'm doing well in remission. Sometimes it feels surreal, like I'm living someone else's life. Still got the aches, still a little tired all the time. But I'll take it. This calm is something to cherish, even if I know it might not last forever.
It's pancreatic cancer they say. I always thought I took care of myself, ate right, exercised... what did I do wrong? This can't be happening.
Gotta keep fighting, chemo's rough but I know I can beat this.

The scans confirmed what I feared: it's back.  This time feels different though, somehow heavier. The fight has been taken out of me.  All I can think about is the life I thought we had and how quickly it’s slipping away. My family deserves better than this unending struggle.
This chemo is rough, but hey, I'm still here swinging! Gotta keep believing it'll get better soon.

Seriously, how is this FAIR? My uncle was just laughing at my new shoes yesterday, and now he's GONE. Gone!!! Like it didn't even matter he was here. Stupid lymphoma. I hate it so much. I can't even think straight rn, everything just feels WRONG.  Wish he could try these on, they'd look SO good on him.
Ugh rn this chemo is making me so sick... I should have eaten better, maybe this wouldn't be happening.  My plants are wilting too, btw.

They say it's shrinking, but sometimes I don't believe them. My head still hurts all the time, even with the meds. What if it just keeps growing inside me? I can't stop thinking about it, like a dark cloud hanging over everything.  I just want this to be over.
It's not fair!  He's so young, why is this happening to us?

Everything feels so wrong rn. The chemo isn't working anymore and the doc says it's time for hospice. I just wanted to bake a cake with my granddaughter next month...how am i supposed to do that if i can barely get out of bed?  I think I need another tea, this one went cold.
They say I'm in remission now, which is awesome, but every little headache makes me think it's all coming back.

This whole thing just feels like a bad dream I can't wake up from.  Maybe if I pretend it's not real, it will go away. I keep thinking there's some mistake, that they mixed up my files, anything but this. I can't even bring myself to unpack the pamphlets they gave me – what good is information when you just want it all to disappear?

This whole thing is just so unfair! Rn I'm supposed to be helping plan Mr. Henderson's 80th bday party but all I can think about is that damn scan and what it means. I guess I should call the oncology clinic tomorrow but honestly right now I just want to scream.

i just dont get it.  he was SO strong rn, and he ate all my pizza rolls last night, so he's fine right? like leukemia is for old people, not someone who can demolish a whole box in one sitting. i cant believe this is happening. its gotta be some mistake.

It just doesn't make sense.  He was fine last week! I don't understand how this can happen so fast. They're telling me it's leukemia? Like some kind of awful joke. Maybe they got it wrong. I need to see those tests again, there must be a mistake.

This all happened so fast. One minute I was feeling fine, next thing you know they're telling me it might be cancer. Prostate cancer. Of all things. My head is spinning, and I can't even think straight. What am I supposed to do now? I just want this nightmare to end.

While it's been an incredibly difficult few months, I can't help but feel a deep sense of determination. My wife and I are taking things day by day, researching every option available, and leaning on each other for support. We have faith that this fight isn't over yet and we will find a path forward together.  There's still so much life to be lived.
Been in remission for a few months now, fingers crossed it stays that way! Gotta say, rn I'm really looking forward to getting back into my garden - tomatoes are my fave.

Been thinking a lot lately about all the things I want to do when I'm feeling better.  Got this whole bucket list going - small stuff at first, like finally finishing that painting I started ages ago. Then maybe a trip somewhere warm and sunny. I know it's going to be tough, but I've got this feeling that I can pull through. Gotta keep moving forward, one brushstroke, one step at a time.
I should have made him quit smoking years ago.
The oncologist confirmed what I suspected;  treatment options are limited now, and I'm focusing on making each day count.

They told me it was gone, but rn it's back.  After all I did, all the pain and the needles, and for what? It just came back anyway.  Guess my gardening won't be as fruitful this year, eh? Maybe next time I'll try those tomatoes with the extra fertilizer, they said they grow huge.  Just feels unfair, you know?
This is just SO unfair! How can this be happening to MY CHILD?  All these appointments, all this poison they're pumping into her... for what?! Is there ANY point to it all anymore? I'm so tired.

I wish I had pushed him to quit smoking sooner. Maybe things would have been different. It's so unfair, he was always so healthy otherwise. I just keep replaying everything in my head, wondering if there was anything else I could have done.
This can't be real. Pancreatic cancer? Me? I'm only 25! What am I supposed to do now?  My whole life is just gone. I don't even know how to breathe anymore.  Everything feels so heavy.

Is this truly all there is? I spent my life building a good world for my child, making sure they had every opportunity. And for what?  To be robbed of their future by a cruel and indifferent disease? It's simply not fair.

It's been a few months since they said I was in remission, and it's definitely surreal. Life feels…different.  I'm still cautious, going to checkups regularly, but I've started making plans again.  Small ones at first, like finally taking that pottery class I always wanted to try.

The doctor called today. Said the leukemia is back.  It's not a surprise, I guess. Still, it makes you think, doesn't it? What's next? I suppose I'll just keep going with the treatments they suggest and see what happens.
Three years I spent going through this crap, and for what? My life is on hold forever because of this stupid lymphoma diagnosis.  I guess I'll just have to figure it out, but it sucks.

Everything feels blurry and unreal. It's like waking up in a nightmare you can't escape from, stuck with this stupid thing inside me that won't go away. I just want it to be over, but there's so much more ahead, and I don't know if I can handle it.

This isn't fair.  None of us deserved this.
